sentence_reviews.text: Continuous waveforms and time–frequency representations encode speech, music, acoustics, and sensor streams.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Reviewed as synthesis across cited source pointers and, where applicable, the canonical atlas graph context.
sentence_reviews.notes: Former ambiguity resolved by narrowing the audit interpretation to synthesis support rather than requiring exact-source wording.

sentence_reviews.text: Encoding choices can discard information, amplify bias, or make important invariances difficult to learn. Its direct dependencies include Signal processing & harmonic analysis, Sequences, events & time series.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Synthesized from cited node sources plus the atlas dependency context in data/atlas.json.
sentence_reviews.notes: Limitation is reviewed as a broad synthesis/tradeoff statement for the family-level node, not as a direct quotation from one source.

sentence_reviews.text: Waveforms, audio & spectrograms consumes, emits, or organizes information using Sequences, events & time series.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: neural networks for sequences and sequence generation/classification (lines 463-478); AIMA contents.html: probabilistic reasoning over time and NLP sequence/language models (lines 361-382 and 621-630).
sentence_reviews.notes: The sources support sequence/time modeling; applying that to waveforms and spectrograms is synthesis.

sentence_reviews.text: Waveforms, audio & spectrograms consumes, emits, or organizes information using Scalars, vectors, matrices & tensors.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: linear algebra data matrices and sequence models (lines 206-259 and 463-504); AIMA contents.html: tensor operations in deep learning and sequence/language models (lines 561-575 and 621-660).
sentence_reviews.notes: The sources support tensor/vector/matrix representations and sequence models, but not audio tensorization in exact wording.

sentence_reviews.text: In this atlas graph, Waveforms, audio & spectrograms is modeled as depending on Signal processing & harmonic analysis, Sequences, events & time series and feeding downstream nodes such as Multimodal, simulated & streaming data, Speech recognition & spoken-language understanding, Speech synthesis, voice & music generation.

edge_reviews.notes: Audio waveforms are naturally sequence/time data, and sequence modeling is directly supported. The represents_with/core edge is defensible.
Vector/matrix/tensor representations are broadly supported for ML sequence models. Common strength is defensible because tensors are representational rather than the audio-domain concept itself.
